what_it_does: Runs a Bash rebuild pipeline from the project root: executes three Node build scripts, then runs proof scripts and lint scripts, printing PASS or FAIL for each check and exiting on the first failure.
observations: Uses set -euo pipefail for stricter shell execution.
Computes ROOT from the script location and changes to that directory's parent.
Runs engine/build/surface.mjs, engine/build/canon.mjs, and factory/build.mjs before validation.
Runs proof checks for resolve and resolve-sequence with stdout suppressed.
Runs lint checks for voice, canon, dna, types, and space with stdout suppressed.
Prints a final success message only after all generation and checks complete.
standouts: Single command combines regeneration and verification.
The header comment names expected generated artifacts and proof categories.
Validation loops use explicit PASS/FAIL reporting with immediate exit on failure.
conventions: Bash script uses a portable shebang and strict mode.
Project root is derived relative to the script path.
Node scripts are invoked by explicit relative paths.
Check names are enumerated inline in compact for-loops.
Successful checks are reported with a PASS prefix.
concerns: The script suppresses check stdout, so failure context may be unavailable from this command alone.
The generated artifact list exists only in comments, not as validated script data.
Node and the referenced scripts are assumed to be available without explicit preflight checks.
notes: This file is the project-local rebuild entrypoint that regenerates derived outputs and gates completion on proofs and lint checks.
questions: Should stderr from the proof and lint scripts be enough diagnostic output when a check fails?
Are the generated artifacts listed in the header intended to be exhaustive?
Should the script verify that required Node scripts exist before running the pipeline?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the behavior shown: it regenerates derived outputs through build scripts, then proves the result by running proof and lint scripts.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make regeneration repeatable and auditable by tying build steps and validation checks into one deterministic command.
novelty: The file combines an executable rebuild pipeline with a comment-level ledger of generated files and proof categories.
contribution: Provides a neutral rebuild-and-verify substrate: a script-local orchestration pattern that keeps derived generation, proof execution, lint execution, and terminal status reporting together.
summary_for_embedding: A strict Bash rebuild script that runs Node generation tasks, then proof and lint checks, reporting PASS or FAIL and exiting on errors.
intention_for_embedding: It was for regenerating all derived project artifacts and confirming the regenerated state through scripted proofs and linters.
